title: Welcome to WLED wiring designer!
dragComponents: Start by dragging components onto the workspace or load one of example diagramms

popover.startConnection: Please select the pin/connector to start from:
popover.closeConnection: Please select the pin/connector to finish:

message.startPinSelected: Source pin/connector is selected. Now please select the target pin/connector.
message.theSamePin: Starting pin/connector is the same as end pin/connector!
message.loadModelSuccess: The WLED wiring model is successfully loaded!
message.loadingModel: Loading wiring model from file link ...
message.loadModelError: The WLED wiring model cannot be loaded. Eventually this link is not valid any more.
message.loadModelWrongLink: The link is wrong! Please check it.
message.componentUpdatesApplied: The updates were applied. Please check this component.
message.componentUpdatesAvailableDescription: {{count}} component(s) in this diagram can be updated to the current template. Update all components now?

components.title: Components
components.updateExplanation: This component in the diagram is older than the current component in the app. The following changes can be transferred.

check.title: Check wiring diagramm
check.comingSoon: Rule-based check will be implemented in next months. Stay tuned!
check.betaNoticeDescription: This diagram check is still under development. Issue detection may be incomplete or incorrect.
check.notChecked: No check has been run yet.
check.noIssuesDescription: The current diagram check did not find any issues.
check.rulesModalDescription: These rules are currently applied when checking the wiring diagram.

diagramEmpty.title: Diagram is empty
diagramEmpty.shortDescription: There are no components in the diagram yet.
diagramEmpty.description: The check can only provide technical guidance once components and wires are present in the diagram.
diagramEmpty.recommendation: Add components first, then run the check again.

network-rules.title: Network rules
network-rules.description: Checks fundamental issues in component-linked nets.

groundMissing.title: No ground net is connected
groundMissing.shortDescription: GND pins exist, but no connected ground net was found.
groundMissing.description: At least one GND pin exists in the diagram, but none of them is wired. Components with a ground reference need a common ground connection.
groundMissing.recommendation: Connect the GND pins of the involved components together.

groundMultiple.title: Multiple separate ground nets
groundMultiple.shortDescription: All GND connections must belong to one common ground net.
groundMultiple.description: {{count}} separate ground nets were found. Parts of the circuit therefore do not share a common reference.
groundMultiple.recommendation: Connect the separate ground nets together.

mainsLowVoltageMixed.title: Mains voltage and low voltage are connected
mainsLowVoltageMixed.shortDescription: A net connects L/N to low-voltage or signal terminals.
mainsLowVoltageMixed.description: The net contains {{classifications}}. Mains voltage must not be connected to GND, DC supply, or signal nets.
mainsLowVoltageMixed.recommendation: Keep mains wiring strictly separated from low-voltage and signal wiring.

peActiveMixed.title: Protective earth is connected to an active conductor
peActiveMixed.shortDescription: A PE net is connected to active supply or a signal net.
peActiveMixed.description: The net contains {{classifications}}. PE must not be connected to L, N, DC supply, or signals.
peActiveMixed.recommendation: Separate PE from active conductors and signal connections.

rs485Mixed.title: RS485 A and B are connected
rs485Mixed.shortDescription: A net connects RS485_A to RS485_B.
rs485Mixed.description: The differential RS485 lines A and B must not be connected to each other.
rs485Mixed.recommendation: Separate RS485_A and RS485_B into two distinct nets.

mixedClassifications.title: Different output types are connected
mixedClassifications.shortDescription: A net contains multiple net classes.
mixedClassifications.description: The net contains {{classifications}} at the same time. This means outputs of different types are connected together.
mixedClassifications.recommendation: Separate the different output types into distinct nets.

multipleSupplySources.title: Multiple independent voltage sources are connected
multipleSupplySources.shortDescription: A supply net contains multiple independent voltage sources.
multipleSupplySources.description: The net contains {{count}} independent voltage sources: {{sources}}.
multipleSupplySources.recommendation: Separate the voltage sources or make sure only forwarded outputs share the same net.

signalSinkWithoutSource.title: {{signal}} input without source
signalSinkWithoutSource.shortDescription: A {{signal}} input is wired, but the net has no matching source.
signalSinkWithoutSource.description: The net contains {{sinks}}, but no matching {{signal}} output.
signalSinkWithoutSource.recommendation: Connect the input to a matching output or check the pin function of the involved components.

digitalSignalVoltageMismatch.title: Digital level does not match
digitalSignalVoltageMismatch.shortDescription: A digital input is driven with an incompatible output voltage.
digitalSignalVoltageMismatch.description: {{input}} allows {{min}} V to {{max}} V, but is connected to incompatible digital outputs: {{sources}}.
digitalSignalVoltageMismatch.recommendation: Use a suitable level shifter or connect the input to a digital output within the allowed voltage range.

multipleSignalSources.title: Multiple {{signal}} sources are connected
multipleSignalSources.recommendation: Separate the outputs so each signal net has only one source.

supplyInputWithoutSource.title: Supply input without voltage source
supplyInputWithoutSource.shortDescription: A supply input is wired, but the net has no voltage source.
supplyInputWithoutSource.description: The net contains supply inputs ({{inputs}}), but no independent suppl_out output.
supplyInputWithoutSource.recommendation: Connect the net to a suitable voltage source.

supplySourceWithoutConsumer.title: Supply net without consumer
supplySourceWithoutConsumer.shortDescription: A supply output is wired, but no supply input is connected.
supplySourceWithoutConsumer.description: The net contains a voltage source, but no recognizable supply consumer.
supplySourceWithoutConsumer.recommendation: Check whether the supply should be routed to a component with a supply input.

supplyVoltageMismatch.title: Supply voltage does not match
supplyVoltageMismatch.shortDescription: The source voltage does not match all supply inputs in the net.
supplyVoltageMismatch.description: The source provides {{voltage}} V, but at least one supply input does not allow this range: {{inputs}}.
supplyVoltageMismatch.recommendation: Adjust the supply voltage or separate components with different voltage ranges.

component-rules.title: Component rules
component-rules.description: Checks fundamental requirements of individual components.

requiredPinUnconnected.title: Required pin is not connected
requiredPinUnconnected.shortDescription: A component has a pin that must be connected.
requiredPinUnconnected.description: {{component}} has required pins without a connection: {{handles}}.
requiredPinUnconnected.recommendation: Connect all pins of this component that are marked as required connections.

groundMissing.title: Component without ground
groundMissing.shortDescription: A component with a GND pin is not connected to ground.
groundMissing.description: {{component}} has GND pins, but none of them is connected to a ground net.
groundMissing.recommendation: Connect at least one GND pin of this component to the common ground net.

powerMissing.title: Component without supply
powerMissing.shortDescription: A component with a supply input is not powered.
powerMissing.description: {{component}} has supply or USB inputs, but no supply input is connected to a supply net containing a suppl_out pin of another component, and no USB terminal is connected to a USB net.
powerMissing.recommendation: Connect a supply input to a suitable external voltage source or connect the USB terminal to a USB supply.

mainsInputMissing.title: {{label}} input is not connected
mainsInputMissing.shortDescription: A component with a {{label}} input is not connected to the matching net.
mainsInputMissing.description: {{component}} has {{label}} inputs, but at least one of them is not connected to a matching {{label}} net.
mainsInputMissing.recommendation: Connect every {{label}} input of this component to the matching {{label}} net.

sn74Ahct125nUsedChannelInputMissing.title: SN74AHCT125N channel input is not driven
sn74Ahct125nUsedChannelInputMissing.shortDescription: A used buffer output has an input without a digital output source or an /OE pin that is neither digitally driven nor tied to GND.
sn74Ahct125nUsedChannelInputMissing.description: {{component}} channel {{channel}} drives a digital input via {{output}}, but these related pins are not connected correctly: {{handles}}. A must be on a net with dig_out; /OE may be on a net with dig_out or GND.
sn74Ahct125nUsedChannelInputMissing.recommendation: Connect the related A pin to a suitable digital output and the /OE pin to a digital output or GND, or disconnect the unused Y output from the digital input.

simulation.title: Simulate current flow
simulation.comingSoon: Simulation of power distribution will be implemented in future. Stay tuned!
simulation.notRun: No simulation has been run yet.
simulation.invalidated: Simulation results were removed because the diagram changed.
simulation.failedDescription: The simulation model could not be created. Check the messages below.

tools.title: Tools
tools.rerouteAllTooltip: Sequentially re-route all wires with Pathfinder
tools.rerouteAllDisabledPF: Only available in Auto/PF connection mode
tools.rerouteAllDisabledEmpty: There are no wires in the diagram
tools.rerouteAllDescription: Routes short component pairs first and keeps wire IDs, connections, and properties.

export.title: Save / export / examples
export.buttonShare: Generate public link to the model

share.modalAttentionText: When you click "OK" your model, along with all the entered data, will be transferred to a public server (https://github.com) and saved there. For this purpose, your model data and your IP address will also be transferred to https://wled-api.myhome-control.de/. You will then be shown a link that you can use to share the model with others.
share.modalLinkError: An error occurred! Please try again if necessary.

legalNotice.title: Legal notice
legalNotice.disputeSettlmentText: Consumer dispute settlement / universal arbitration board: We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute settlement proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

dataPrivacy.title: Data privacy
dataPrivacy.text1: This page is hosted and operated as part of 'Github Pages'. It is possible and probable that Github collects and processes personal data, which then also affects visitors of the wled-compile.github.io web page. We have, however, no influence on it. Please read the Github privacy statement:
dataPrivacy.text2: In addition, as soon as you use 'Share' functionality, your IP address, the model you created and all the data you entered forwarded to https://myhome-control.de and to https://github.com/wled-development/wled-wiring-store. This is absolutely necessary for this functionality. The data protection declaration for https://myhome-control.de can be found at
dataPrivacy.text3: Our website contains links to external websites over which we have no control. We cannot accept any liability for this third-party content. The respective provider or operator of the pages is always responsible for the content of the linked pages. The linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognizable at that time. If we become aware of any legal violations, we will remove the relevant link immediately.

links.title: Useful links
links.link5Text: Compile your own WELD SW online:

contribute.title: Contribute/GitHub
contribute.text: Contributions are highly appreciated! If you'd like to contribute, please follow the project's GitHub link:

examples.example1: ESP32 uC + Level Shifter + DC/DC + 24V LED strip
examples.example3: ABC! WLED Controller + long 24V LED Strip + LineIn + Ethernet
examples.example4: ABC! WLED Controller + Long Range Extension
examples.example5: ABC! WLED Controller + Anal. 24V LED Strip + PWM Board

Button.name: Push Button
Button.description: A generic push button. When pressed and held, the contact is closed. The contact opens as soon as the button is released. Not to be confused with a switch.

DC_JACK_FEMALE.name: DC Connector
DC_JACK_FEMALE.description: Used to split a power supply with a DC connector into a + and - wire.

Elko.name: Electrolytic Capacitor
Elko.description: An electrolytic capacitor with selectable capacitance. Pay attention to polarity! Often used to stabilize the power supply for LEDs or other components.

ESP32_30P.name: ESP32 uC 30 Pins
ESP32_30P.description: ESP32 controller, version with 30 pins. Also known as NodeMCU.

ESP32_38P.name: ESP32 uC 38 Pins
ESP32_38P.description: ESP32 controller, version with 38 pins. Also known as NodeMCU.

ESP32C3D1mini.name: ESP32-C3 D1 mini
ESP32C3D1mini.description: ESP32-C3 controller in D1 mini format. WLED can run on this controller type with limitations.

ESP32C3_supermini.name: ESP32-C3 supermini
ESP32C3_supermini.description: ESP32-C3 controller, supermini. WLED can run on this controller type with limitations.

ESP32D1mini.name: ESP32 D1 mini
ESP32D1mini.description: ESP32 controller in D1 mini format.

ESP32S3D1mini.name: ESP32-S3 D1 mini
ESP32S3D1mini.description: ESP32-S3 controller in D1 mini format. WLED can run on this controller type with limitations.

ESP8266D1mini.name: ESP8266 D1 mini
ESP8266D1mini.description: ESP8266 controller in D1 mini format. No longer recommended for new WLED projects (too weak).

InfoNode.name: Text
InfoNode.description: A text element to add labels or notes to the diagram.

INMP441.name: INMP441 microphone
INMP441.description: INMP441 microphone. It is a digital microphone supported by WLED for sound-reactive effects and delivers high quality.

IR_KY022.name: KY022 IR receiver
IR_KY022.description: KY-022 infrared receiver, supported by WLED, allows controlling WLED with an IR remote.

IR_TSOP38238.name: TSOP38238 IR Receiver
IR_TSOP38238.description: TSOP38238 infrared receiver, supported by WLED, allows controlling WLED with an IR remote.

Kerko.name: Ceramic Capacitor
Kerko.description: Ceramic capacitor with selectable capacitance. Commonly used at the input of switching electronic circuits (between power and ground) to suppress noise. Should be connected using the shortest possible leads/traces.

LineBoxNode.name: Rectangle
LineBoxNode.description: An element to draw a rectangle (filled or empty).

miniOTOFuse.name: Melting Fuse
miniOTOFuse.description: A fuse from the automotive sector. Ideal for voltages up to 32V. Only buy from specialized dealers, as many cheap copies on marketplaces do not function properly. WLED calculator (www.wled-calculator.github.io) can be used for dimensioning.

PIR_HCSR501.name: HC-SR501 PIR Sensor
PIR_HCSR501.description: PIR motion sensor of type HCSR501. Can be used with WLED.

PLUG_LNPE.name: Power Plug
PLUG_LNPE.description: Plug for 230V power outlets. Warning: Risk of death! Only use if you have appropriate knowledge.

PSU_HP.name: Power supply
PSU_HP.description: Power supply with selectable voltage/current (power). These are typically high-performance supplies used for larger setups. Power plug must be connected separately.

PSU_USB.name: USB Power Supply
PSU_USB.description: USB power supply with 5V output voltage and selectable current up to 5A.

PSU_USB_WIRES.name: USB Power Supply
PSU_USB_WIRES.description: USB power supply with 5V output voltage, selectable current up to 5A, and separate +/- wires.

SN74AHCT125N.name: Level Shifter
SN74AHCT125N.description: A level shifter type recommended for WLED. A level shifter is necessary to convert the microcontroller's control signal (3.3V) to the LED data input (5V) level for addressable LEDs. Without a level shifter, flickering often occurs (almost always with 12V and 24V LEDs).

SolderJoint.name: Solder Joint
SolderJoint.description: Representation of a solder joint, e.g., to connect wires together.

WAGO_2X.name: WAGO Connector 2x
WAGO_2X.description: Widely used spring clamp connector, two-way.

WAGO_3X.name: WAGO Connector 3x
WAGO_3X.description: Widely used spring clamp connector, three-way.

WS2812B_5V_30LPM.name: WS2812B RGB
WS2812B_5V_30LPM.description: WS2812 RGB LED strip, 5V, 30 LEDs per meter, individually addressable LEDs.

MHC_V43.name: WLED V43 Controller
MHC_V43.description: A professional WLED controller for 5V, 12V or 24V addressable LEDs. Includes ESP32 microcontroller, SN74AHCT level shifter, fuse, capacitor, EMI filter. Optional accessories include Ethernet adapter, INMP441 microphone, line-in adapter, and USB adapter.

MHC_V63.name: WLED V63 Shield
MHC_V63.description: A professional WLED shield for 5V or 12V addressable LEDs. Includes SN74AHCT level shifter, fuse, capacitor. Optional accessories include INMP441 microphone, line-in adapter, and USB adapter. Can be used with ESP8266, ESP32, ESP32-C3, or ESP32-S3 in D1 mini format.

MHC_SwitchBoard.name: WLED switch board
MHC_SwitchBoard.description: A better/modern alternative to relais board for switching LEDs completely off. Overcurrent, overtemperature, short circuit, and reverse polarity protection.

MHC_PWMBoard.name: WLED PWM board
MHC_PWMBoard.description: The board uses MOSFETs to control analog LED strips with PWM signals. 3 channels, each max. 5A. Overcurrent, overtemperature, short circuit, and reverse polarity protection.

MHC_RS485_R.name: RS-485 receiver
MHC_RS485_R.description: RS-485 receiver for use as a range extender receiver with the RS-485 adapter.

MHC_Relay5V.name: 5V Relay Board
MHC_Relay5V.description: A professional relay board for use with 5V addressable LEDs. Allows turning off the LED strips when not in use via relays, saving energy, extending LED strip life, and increasing safety. Two paths allow two separate controls or, e.g., control of two power inputs.

MHC_Relay12V.name: 12V Relay Board
MHC_Relay12V.description: A professional relay board for use with 12V addressable LEDs. Allows turning off the LED strips when not in use via relays, saving energy, extending LED strip life, and increasing safety. Two paths allow two separate controls or, e.g., control of two power inputs.

MHC_Relay24V.name: 24V Relay Board
MHC_Relay24V.description: A professional relay board for use with 24V addressable LEDs. Allows turning off the LED strips when not in use via relays, saving energy, extending LED strip life, and increasing safety. Two paths allow two separate controls or, e.g., control of two power inputs.

LM2596_PCB.name: LM2596 DC/DC
LM2596_PCB.description: DC/DC (step-down) converter with adjustable output voltage.

IRLZ44N.name: IRLZ44N
IRLZ44N.description: MOSFET. This type is recommended for use with WLED and can be used to control analog LED strips.

DCDC_mini.name: DC/DC Mini
DCDC_mini.description: DC/DC (step-down) converter with adjustable output voltage.

WS2814_24V_60LPM.name: WS2814 RGBW
WS2814_24V_60LPM.description: RGBW LED strip, individually addressable LEDs in groups of 6 (1 logical LED = 6 physical).

WS2814_12V_30LPM.name: WS2814 RGBW
WS2814_12V_30LPM.description: RGBW LED strip, individually addressable LEDs in groups of 3 (1 logical LED = 3 physical).

WS2818_12V_30LPM.name: WS2818 RGB
WS2818_12V_30LPM.description: RGB LED strip, individually addressable LEDs in groups of 3 (1 logical LED = 3 physical).

FCOB_12V_720LPM.name: FCOB RGB
FCOB_12V_720LPM.description: FCOB RGB LED strip, 720 LEDs/m (240 LEDs each color per meter, 20 logical LEDs/m)

FCOB_24V_720LPM.name: FCOB RGB
FCOB_24V_720LPM.description: FCOB RGB LED strip, 720 LEDs/m (240 LEDs each color per meter, 20 logical LEDs/m)

FCOB_24V_784LPM.name: FCOB RGBW
FCOB_24V_784LPM.description: FCOB RGBW LED strip, 784 LEDs/m (196 LEDs each color per meter, 14 logical LEDs/m)

APA102_5V_30LPM.name: APA102 RGB
APA102_5V_30LPM.description: APA102 LED strip with clock signal, 5V, 30 LEDs/Meter, individually addressable LEDs.

AN_WHITE_24V_240LPM.name: Analogue white
AN_WHITE_24V_240LPM.description: Analogues LED strip (white 2835 LEDs), 24V, 240 LEDs/m, 2.5 cm segments (6 LEDs)

AN_RGB_CCT_48V_90LPM.name: Anal. RGB CCT
AN_RGB_CCT_48V_90LPM.description: Analogues RGB CCT LED strip (5050 LEDs), 48V, 90 LEDs/m, segemnts of 16.65 cm (15 LEDs)

AN_RGB_24V_120LPM.name: Anal. RGB
AN_RGB_24V_120LPM.description: Analogues RGB LED strip (2835 LEDs), 24V, 120 LEDs/m, segments of 5 cm (6 LEDs)

WS2805_24V_60LPM.name: WS2805 RGBW CCT
WS2805_24V_60LPM.description: RGBW CCT LED strip (digital), individually addressable LEDs in groups of 6 (1 logical LED = 6 physical).

WS2805_12V_60LPM.name: WS2805 RGBW CCT
WS2805_12V_60LPM.description: RGBW CCT LED strip (digital), individually addressable LEDs in groups of 6 (1 logical LED = 6 physical).
